title: Valerianella dentata
taxonomy: Valerianella dentata is a species of plant that belongs to the family Valerianaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as valerian or centranthus. Within the Valerianaceae family, Valerianella dentata is classified as a genus of small, annual or biennial plants. The plant is native to Europe and Asia and can be found growing in a variety of habitats. As a member of the Valerianaceae family, Valerianella dentata shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as its small white flowers.
uses: Valerianella dentata is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves have been used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. In some cultures, Valerianella dentata is also used as a diuretic to help with urinary issues. While more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness, the plant has been traditionally used to support digestive health.

intro: Valerianella dentata, also known as Corn Salad or Lamb's Lettuce, is a small, delicate annual plant with edible leaves and stems.
